_comment: Scenario-facing Michigan trauma/environmental placement for suspected pediatric non-accidental trauma. Source material: Michigan MDHHS Procedure 7-2 Child Abuse and Neglect (Revised 5/23/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023) and MCL 722.623. Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
condition: Child Abuse and Neglect — Mandatory Reporting
protocol_reference: Michigan Procedure Protocol Section 7-2: Child Abuse and Neglect — MDHHS Approved 5/23/2023; MCL 722.623
purpose: Guide EMS recognition, objective documentation, patient-centered care, and mandatory reporting for suspected child abuse or neglect. EMS personnel are mandatory reporters and must report reasonable suspicion directly to DHHS.

sections.title: Legal Obligations
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 7-2: Child Abuse — Legal Obligations (MCL 722.623)
points: All EMS personnel are mandatory reporters of suspected child abuse or neglect under MCL 722.623
Reasonable suspicion is the reporting threshold; EMS providers do not need certainty or proof
Reporting requirement applies even if the patient is not transported
Call 855-444-3911 immediately to report suspected abuse or neglect
Written DHS-3200 form must be completed within 72 hours of the initial verbal report
Reporting to a supervisor, receiving facility, or EMS organization alone does not fulfill the individual provider's mandatory reporting obligation
Do not confront or accuse suspected abusers at the scene

sections.title: Indicators of Possible Child Abuse or Neglect
points: Injury inconsistent with stated mechanism of injury or developmental stage of the child
Bruising in non-ambulatory children or bruising in concerning distributions such as torso, ears, neck, frenulum, angle of jaw, cheeks, eyelids, or subconjunctivae
Pattern injuries suggesting use of objects such as a belt, cord, hand, or bite
Multiple injuries in various stages of healing
Fractures, burns, or soft-tissue injuries inconsistent with the stated mechanism
Delayed presentation for medical care
Caregiver providing inconsistent, changing, evasive, or developmentally implausible histories
Indicators of neglect: malnourishment, unsafe living environment, lack of appropriate clothing or supervision, medical neglect, or abandonment concerns

sections.title: Scene, Patient Care, and Documentation
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 7-2: Child Abuse — Scene and Documentation
points: Primary duty is emergency medical care — treat and stabilize per the appropriate clinical protocol
Maintain scene safety and request law enforcement if safety, custody, or interference concerns are present
Do not confront, accuse, investigate, or interrogate suspected abusers at the scene
When possible and safe, assess the child in a calm, age-appropriate manner and document the child's demeanor, affect, and behavior
Document verbatim statements or excited utterances by the child or caregiver using quotation marks
Document caregiver(s) present and their stated relationship to the child
Document injuries objectively with location, size, color, shape, pattern, tenderness, swelling, and functional impact when observed
Document the physical environment when relevant to suspicion of neglect
Do not delay transport to investigate or complete documentation

sections.title: Transport and Reporting Coordination
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 7-2: Child Abuse — Transport and Reporting
points: Transport to an appropriate emergency department when medical evaluation is indicated or the child may be unsafe remaining on scene
Communicate objective findings, statements, inconsistent history, patient behavior, and mandatory-reporting plan in handoff
If the patient is not transported, the mandatory report is still required
Coordinate with law enforcement for immediate safety concerns while preserving EMS's independent medical-care and reporting duties

deterioration_flags: Life-threatening injuries — stabilize and transport per the appropriate treatment protocol; mandatory report remains required
Child in immediate danger at scene — coordinate with law enforcement for scene safety while maintaining patient care
Caregiver interfering with care or transport — request law enforcement and prioritize patient safety
Changing or implausible caregiver history combined with concerning injury pattern — treat as reasonable suspicion and complete mandatory report
scope_notes: All EMS provider levels are mandatory reporters under MCL 722.623
Mandatory report hotline: 855-444-3911 — call immediately upon suspicion
Written DHS-3200 form required within 72 hours of verbal report
Reporting to a supervisor, receiving facility, or EMS organization alone does not fulfill the individual legal obligation
Report is required even when patient is not transported
Do not confront suspected abusers — that is a law enforcement/investigative function
